name: Indoor Birdies
about: Indoor Birdies is a members-only indoor golf simulator club located in Jupiter, FL. The venue features a single, private bay equipped with a Trackman 4 launch monitor, offering an exclusive environment for focused practice and gameplay. Memberships are required, with options ranging from 3 to unlimited hours of play per month, and bookings can be made up to 8 days in advance. The facility is designed for golfers seeking a premium, distraction-free experience without the constraints of weather or time.
why_golfers_like_it: Exclusive access to a private Trackman 4 bay with no waiting or distractions
Premium dual radar technology providing ultra-accurate swing and ball tracking data
Flexible 24/7 booking availability for members, up to 8 days in advance
Membership options tailored to different practice needs, from 3 to unlimited hours

food_and_drink.notes: No mention of on-site food service; members can likely bring their own snacks
No mention of alcohol service; venue appears to be focused on golf simulation

simulator_hardware.notes: Dual radar technology providing ultra-accurate swing and ball tracking

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own golf clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, you are required to bring your own clubs to the indoor golf studio. Using your personal set is recommended for the best experience and comfort with your equipment.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.answer: The facility does not mention on-site food or drink service. While it's not explicitly prohibited, the venue appears to be focused on golf simulation, and members are encouraged to wear comfortable clothing and appropriate footwear.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The website does not provide specific information on the time required to play 18 holes. Trackman 4 simulators offer a library of world-famous courses, allowing members to play at their own pace.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: No, Indoor Birdies is a members-only club focused on a serious golfing experience. It is not designed as a family-friendly or kid-oriented venue, and there are no mentions of junior programs or youth-friendly activities.
